mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate decline periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive equity returns over the next month during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of late-cycle expansion observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP signals immediate recession risk and earnings downgrade expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price indices combined with stagnant federal funds rates compress real yields and signal impending monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth alongside flat FEDFUNDS yields positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll employment growth above 200,000 signals robust labor market absorption, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during post-recession recovery phases.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases exceeding 200,000 fail to predict positive 20-day forward returns in > 50% of recovery phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side momentum and inventory restocking, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show negative mean during months with positive INDPRO changes with statistical significance (p < 0.05).
